subject: 🏃 Sunday — Rest Day, Chassis Build Starts Tomorrow
html:
Today's workout: Rest. No scheduled training. Tomorrow kicks off Week 1 of the 4-week Chassis Build phase, so today is about recovery and prep.

Your knee has flagged Yellow three times in the last week (April 12 post-Cambria, April 13 afternoon, April 16 post-run). That's exactly why the PT-driven Chassis Build exists — we're spending the next four weeks fixing the upstream hip and core issues that are loading that knee. No tempo, no long runs, no racing until the PT clears you on May 15.

Tomorrow's preview: Hip & Core A + Upper Push (~60 min). Start with the 12-min daily mobility flush (foam roller T-spine extensions, standing lunges with hip IR, world's greatest stretch, 90/90s, couch stretch, hip CARs, cat-cow). Then Hip & Core A — everything bodyweight for Week 1. Learn the movements: 90/90 PAILs/RAILs, clamshells, single-leg glute bridges, hip airplanes, Copenhagen planks, dead bugs with banded pulldown, chin tuck lifts, Pallof press. Finish with wall sits and TKE on the right leg only. Then Upper Push.

Focus cue: If you do one thing today, do the 12-minute daily mobility flush. Get familiar with it — it runs every single day for the next four weeks. Both sides, equal work on every bilateral exercise.

Context: Day 0 of 14 weeks. 98 days to race day. The next 4 weeks build the chassis so the engine has something to run on. Every session, rate the knee Green/Yellow/Red — that's the primary signal driving this whole plan.
